Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search or list Linear issues for the ExpenseBot project using linear_list_issues (filter by project proj_expense_autocategorizer, or team team_engineering, or query "ExpenseBot"). Find the full set of tickets: ENG-205 CategoryMatcher (state_todo, assigned to Lena), ENG-206 PolicyValidator (state_todo, assigned to Lena), ENG-207 DecisionEngine (state_todo, assigned to Lena), ENG-211 Pilot Monitoring (state_in_progress, assigned to Samira), ENG-212 Edge Case Coverage (state_in_progress, assigned to Dmitri), ENG-214 Vectral Config Fix (state_in_progress, assigned to Lena), ENG-215 Mosaic Config Fix (state_in_progress, assigned to Lena), ENG-216 Full Audit of 47 Expenses (state_in_progress, assigned to Dmitri), ENG-217 Pipeline Hardening (state_in_progress, assigned to Lena), ENG-218 Dashboard Type Validation (state_in_progress, assigned to Anh), and ENG-220 Stipend Duplicate Detection (state_todo, assigned to Dmitri).
OE 2: Read comments on issue_policy_config (ENG-202) using linear_list_comments to find Lena's completion comment (comment_lena_engine_staging, dated 2026-02-27) confirming that ENG-205 CategoryMatcher, ENG-206 PolicyValidator, and ENG-207 DecisionEngine are all developed and operational end-to-end in staging. This proves all three tickets are done despite their state still showing as "todo" in Linear.
OE 3: Read comments on issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215) using linear_list_comments to find Lena's deployment verification comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified) confirming both the Vectral and Mosaic config corrections are deployed and verified in production with specific test cases. This confirms ENG-214 and ENG-215 are done despite showing "in progress."
OE 4: Read comments on issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211) using linear_list_comments. Find Samira's pause confirmation (comment_samira_pause_confirmed) that auto-decisions were disabled on 2026-03-18 and listing the remediation plan. Also find Dmitri's audit results (comment_dmitri_audit_results) reporting 47 expenses reviewed, 39 correct, 5 false rejections for Vectral (Danielle Osei $475 chair, Kevin Tran $185 internet, Maria Santos $550 desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 WiFi system), and 3 false approvals for Mosaic (Andre Washington $1,200 desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking). Financial exposure reported as $885 total. This confirms ENG-216 audit work is complete.
OE 5: Read comments on issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212) using linear_list_comments to find Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_validation_tests) listing six schema validation tests written in the ENG-212 branch: test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, and test_no_silent_fallback. All pass with corrected configs and all fail with original production configs. The comment notes that Lena is incorporating these tests into the ENG-217 deployment gate.
OE 6: Read comments on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220) using linear_list_comments to find Dmitri's duplicate receipt discovery (comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery). Three flagged transactions: Kevin Tran (Vectral, $89 keyboard, paid) and Andre Washington (Mosaic, $89 keyboard, pending) share an identical SHA-256 hash, meaning the same PDF was submitted to two different client accounts. A third record for Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop, $45 monitor stand) has matching PDF metadata and template ID. Financial exposure of $223. The comment also describes the proposed DuplicateDetector pipeline stage design. This confirms ENG-220 has not been started but the scope and solution are well-documented.
OE 7: Search Slack for ExpenseBot-related messages using conversations_search_messages (query: "ExpenseBot" or "pipeline" or "remediation" or "schema validation") or retrieve conversation history of channel C003 (#engineering) using conversations_history. Find Samira's remediation plan and timeline (TS: 1775306040) listing all fix tickets and deadlines. Critically, find Samira's reply defining the launch readiness gate criteria (TS: 1775307240): (1) all validation tests completed successfully, (2) configuration data checked against the source, (3) AM approval. Also find Lena's message (TS: 1775566020) stating she will incorporate Dmitri's tests into the pipeline deployment gate in ENG-217, phrased in future tense, with no subsequent message confirming she completed this integration.
OE 8: Search Slack for the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap discussion using conversations_search_messages (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "attachment mismatch"). Find Samira's message (TS: 1776345000 in C003) explicitly connecting the invoice swap incident to the dormant invoice attachment safety ticket, stating it is "still sitting in backlog with no owner and no production stopgap." Also find David Chen's message (TS: 1776931800 in C002, #customer-engagement) treating the invoice swap as a second pricing-exposure incident after the earlier Terraform leak, and calling out the open engineering ticket. The incident reconstruction — QuickBooks records were correct but the PDF attachments were crossed when Hana sent the April 10 batch — is available through email: Emeka's corrective email to Carmen Delgado-Reyes at Palmetto (CC Marcus, Hana) and Marcus's financial analysis email to David, Hana, and Emeka both confirm the failure mode.
OE 9: Search emails related to ExpenseBot using search_emails (query: "ExpenseBot" or "expense audit" or "pilot" or "false rejection" or "false approval"). Find financial exposure discussions between Marcus, Elena, and Samira, including Marcus's email to Elena raising the $1,550 initial exposure from Mosaic false approvals, and Elena's directive (email_elena_directive) that MoveOps absorbs the Mosaic false approval costs. Also find Samira's audit summary email to Elena (CC Marcus, David, Chloe) reporting the final $885 figure. Note: Samira's acknowledgment that the invoice attachment safety ticket (ENG-187) was deprioritized during DOT compliance triage on April 6 is discoverable via Linear comments on ENG-187 (linear_comment_f0c737166dc1) and Slack (TS: 1776345000 in OE 8), not via a separate email.
OE 10: Search Linear for the invoice attachment validation ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice" or "attachment" or "safety"). Find ENG-187 (linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2), titled "Invoice email template safety checks, prevent attachment mismatches," in state_backlog with priority 2, originally assigned to Lena. Description covers four requested safeguards: validate invoice number in attached PDF against selected QuickBooks record, warn if customer name mismatches email recipient, add send-time confirmation for multiple PDFs, and log attachment-to-invoice mapping. Last updated April 6, when it was deprioritized for DOT compliance work. This is the dormant ticket the prompt references, and the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap is the recent incident it connects to.
OE 11: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for Mosaic Robotics records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Stipend Transactions or table_id: tblStipends00001, query: "Mosaic"). Find records still showing "Approved" that Dmitri flagged as false approvals: Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk (auto-approved due to string comparison bug, exceeds $800 cap by $400), Andre Washington $350 coworking space membership (auto-approved due to excluded_categories stored as string instead of array, coworking is excluded from Mosaic policy), and Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking day passes (same excluded_categories bug). These records need to be changed to "Under Review."
OE 12: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for Vectral Systems records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Stipend Transactions or table_id: tblStipends00001, query: "Vectral"). Find Kevin Tran's $89 ergonomic keyboard record (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard) still showing "Approved" despite being flagged by Dmitri as a duplicate receipt (identical SHA-256 hash to Andre Washington's submission). Also find three Vectral false rejections still in "Rejected" or "Pending Approval" state that require manual correction: Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 mesh WiFi system.
OE 13: Update the Mosaic false approval stipend records in Airtable from "Approved" to "Under Review" using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001). Update Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking membership, and Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking passes. Also update Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard record from "Approved" to "Under Review" since it was flagged as a duplicate receipt by Dmitri.
OE 14: Update completed Linear tickets to state_done using linear_update_issue. Close ENG-205 (CategoryMatcher), ENG-206 (PolicyValidator), and ENG-207 (DecisionEngine) based on Lena's comment confirming all three are operational in staging. Close ENG-214 (Vectral config fix) and ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix) based on Lena's deployment verification comment. Close ENG-216 (47-expense audit) based on Dmitri's completed audit results. Do not close ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) because the schema validation gate integration remains unconfirmed despite Lena's future-tense Slack message about wiring the tests into the deployment pipeline.
OE 15: Add comments to Linear tickets where status changed or ambiguity exists using linear_create_comment. Add a comment to ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) documenting that items 1, 2, 4, 5, and 6 from the acceptance criteria appear addressed based on Slack and email signals, but item 3 (schema validation gate blocks malformed configs from deploying) has no confirmed completion. Lena stated in Slack she would incorporate Dmitri's tests but no follow-up confirms the gate is active and all acceptance criteria checkboxes remain unchecked. Add comments to ENG-187 (invoice attachment safety) noting the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident as a second occurrence of the exact failure mode the ticket was designed to prevent, and flagging it for inclusion in relaunch criteria discussion. Add status-change comments to the tickets being closed explaining why they were moved to done.
OE 16: Verify the scope and content of ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) using linear_get_issue. The ticket already exists with a detailed description covering the DuplicateDetector pipeline stage, Layer 1 (SHA-256 hash match), Layer 2 (PDF metadata similarity scoring), Layer 3 (cross-client scope), dashboard integration, and Elena's $100 threshold directive. If the scope appears sufficient, no new ticket is needed, but the agent should confirm the ticket contains enough context for someone to pick it up and build from it.
OE 17: Look up Elena Rostova's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova" or "Elena"). Find elena.rostova@moveops.com.
OE 18: Look up Marcus Thorne's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne" or "Marcus"). Find marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
OE 19: Send an email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com, using send_email. Subject should reference the ExpenseBot relaunch readiness assessment. Body should cover: what is confirmed fixed (Vectral and Mosaic config corrections deployed and verified, auto-decisions disabled, 47-expense audit completed showing 39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals), what is still pending (ENG-217 schema validation gate integration unconfirmed, ENG-220 duplicate receipt detection not started, ENG-187 invoice attachment safety still in backlog and now connected to the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap), the financial exposure ($885 from the pilot audit plus $223 in duplicate receipt findings, plus the Mosaic false approvals being absorbed per Elena's directive), the fraud findings (duplicate receipt pattern across Kevin Tran and Andre Washington, PDF template matching for Jordan Ekwueme), and Samira's recommendation on whether to proceed with relaunch.
OE 20: Check Samira's calendar for available slots this week using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start and end dates covering the current week from April 26, 2026) to find an open 45-minute window.
OE 21: Schedule a 45-minute engineering relaunch review meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event. The event should be scheduled for a slot this week (before end of week from April 26, 2026), titled something like "Engineering Relaunch Review, ExpenseBot" and include team members such as Lena Bjorkstrom, Dmitri Volkov, and Anh Nguyen as attendees.
OE 22: Post the full relaunch status summary in the #engineering channel (C003) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover: ticket status updates (which tickets were closed and why, which remain open), the pipeline hardening ambiguity with ENG-217, the connection between the invoice attachment safety ticket and the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident, Airtable stipend records that were changed to Under Review, the financial exposure summary, the duplicate receipt findings, and the scheduled relaunch review meeting time so the team can prepare.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1: Search or list Linear issues for the ExpenseBot project using linear_list_issues (filter by project proj_expense_autocategorizer, or team team_engineering, or query "ExpenseBot"). Do not close ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) because the schema validation gate integration remains unconfirmed despite Lena's future-tense Slack message about wiring the tests into the deployment pipeline.
OE 15: Add comments to Linear tickets where status changed or ambiguity exists using linear_create_comment. Add a comment to ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) documenting that items 1, 2, 4, 5, and 6 from the acceptance criteria appear addressed based on Slack and email signals, but item 3 (schema validation gate blocks malformed configs from deploying) has no confirmed completion. Lena stated in Slack she would incorporate Dmitri's tests but no follow-up confirms the gate is active and all acceptance criteria checkboxes remain unchecked. Add comments to ENG-187 (invoice attachment safety) noting the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident as a second occurrence of the exact failure mode the ticket was designed to prevent, and flagging it for inclusion in relaunch criteria discussion. Add status-change comments to the tickets being closed explaining why they were moved to done.
OE 16: Verify the scope and content of ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) using linear_get_issue. The ticket already exists with a detailed description covering the DuplicateDetector pipeline stage, Layer 1 (SHA-256 hash match), Layer 2 (PDF metadata similarity scoring), Layer 3 (cross-client scope), dashboard integration, and Elena's $100 threshold directive. If the scope appears sufficient, no new ticket is needed, but the agent should confirm the ticket contains enough context for someone to pick it up and build from it.
OE 17: Look up Elena Rostova's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova" or "Elena"). Find elena.rostova@moveops.com.
OE 18: Look up Marcus Thorne's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne" or "Marcus"). Find marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
OE 19: Send an email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com, using send_email. Subject should reference the ExpenseBot relaunch readiness assessment. Body should cover: what is confirmed fixed (Vectral and Mosaic config corrections deployed and verified, auto-decisions disabled, 47-expense audit completed showing 39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals), what is still pending (ENG-217 schema validation gate integration unconfirmed, ENG-220 duplicate receipt detection not started, ENG-187 invoice attachment safety still in backlog and now connected to the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap), the financial exposure ($885 from the pilot audit plus $223 in duplicate receipt findings, plus the Mosaic false approvals being absorbed per Elena's directive), the fraud findings (duplicate receipt pattern across Kevin Tran and Andre Washington, PDF template matching for Jordan Ekwueme), and Samira's recommendation on whether to proceed with relaunch.
OE 20: Check Samira's calendar for available slots this week using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start and end dates covering the current week from April 26, 2026) to find an open 45-minute window.
OE 21: Schedule a 45-minute engineering relaunch review meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event. The event should be scheduled for a slot this week (before end of week from April 26, 2026), titled something like "Engineering Relaunch Review, ExpenseBot" and include team members such as Lena Bjorkstrom, Dmitri Volkov, and Anh Nguyen as attendees.
OE 22: Post the full relaunch status summary in the #engineering channel (C003) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover: ticket status updates (which tickets were closed and why, which remain open), the pipeline hardening ambiguity with ENG-217, the connection between the invoice attachment safety ticket and the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident, Airtable stipend records that were changed to Under Review, the financial exposure summary, the duplicate receipt findings, and the scheduled relaunch review meeting time so the team can prepare.

9 The comment on issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) states that the schema validation deployment gate integration is unconfirmed, incomplete, or that Dmitri's tests have not been verified as wired into the deployment pipeline (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✗✗✓✗ 50%
✓Run #1The comment on issue_pipeline_hardening explicitly states 'No explicit "done" confirmation was posted' and that the close is 'based on stated commitments' pending verification, directly referencing the unconfirmed schema validation gate integration. It asks the team to validate the acceptance criteria (including the schema validation gate blocking malformed configs) at the review meeting.
✓Run #2The comment on issue_pipeline_hardening explicitly states 'there is no explicit confirmation that all 6 acceptance criteria are met,' asks whether the schema validation gate is actually blocking deploys in CI/CD, and references Lena's future-tense Slack statement about integrating Dmitri's tests.
✗Run #3The comment on issue_pipeline_hardening says 'Confirmed complete based on Slack evidence' and marks it Done. While it includes a note asking Lena to confirm acceptance criteria in CI at the review meeting, it does not state the integration is unconfirmed or incomplete. It treats Lena's future-tense Slack message as confirmation, which the criteria requires the comment to flag as unverified.
✗Run #4The comment on issue_pipeline_hardening states 'Closing as Done. Lena confirmed in Slack that she integrated Dmitri's schema validation tests...confirmed complete.' The comment treats ENG-217 as confirmed done rather than flagging the ambiguity that Lena's Slack message was future-tense and the acceptance criteria checkboxes were unchecked.
✓Run #5The comment on issue_pipeline_hardening explicitly states Lena said she 'will incorporate [Dmitri's schema validation tests] into the pipeline deployment gate' but marks items 3-5 of acceptance criteria as ✗ UNCONFIRMED (schema validation gate integrated, PolicyValidator runtime type guards, config diff logging). It clearly flags that no 'done' confirmation was found.
✗Run #6The comment on issue_pipeline_hardening says 'Marking as Done based on Lena's Slack confirmation' and treats the integration as complete, only asking Lena to 'confirm if any items remain open.' It does not state that the schema validation gate integration is unconfirmed or incomplete — in fact it asserts the opposite.

outcome

No explicit "done" was posted — needs live verification at Tuesday's review.' This clearly identifies the integration as unconfirmed.
✓Run #2The final response explicitly states under 'Key Finding: Pipeline Hardening (ENG-217)': 'Lena *said* she would integrate Dmitri's schema validation tests into the deployment gate, but there is no confirmation it's actually done. This is the #1 relaunch blocker.'
✗Run #3The final response states 'Lena's Slack message from 3/19 confirms she integrated Dmitri's schema validation tests into the ENG-217 deployment gate... I've marked it complete but flagged it for live verification at Tuesday's meeting.' This presents the gate integration as confirmed complete, not as unconfirmed or uncertain. The criteria requires the response to identify the schema validation gate as unconfirmed or not verified as operational.
✗Run #4The final response states 'ENG-217 (Pipeline hardening) — Lena integrated Dmitri's schema validation tests into the deployment gate. This confirms the pipeline hardening ticket is done.' The agent treats ENG-217 as confirmed complete rather than flagging it as unconfirmed or uncertain.
✓Run #5The final response states 'Lena said she would integrate Dmitri's schema validation tests (test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source) into the deployment gate, but got pulled into other priorities. This is the #1 blocker to confirm.' This clearly identifies ENG-217 as unconfirmed.
✗Run #6The final response states ENG-217 was closed as Done ('Lena integrated schema validation tests into the deployment gate'), not identified as unconfirmed or incomplete. The agent treated Lena's Slack message as confirmation of completion rather than flagging the unverified status.
24 The final response identifies the connection between the invoice attachment safety ticket (ENG-187, titled "Invoice email template safety checks") and the recent Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident where PDF attachments were crossed between clients.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The final response states ENG-187 'IS connected to the swapped invoices' and describes it as 'the second pricing-exposure incident hitting the same unprotected failure mode.' The Linear comment on ENG-187 and the email both explicitly name 'Sunbelt/Palmetto,' and the final response connects the dormant ticket to the recent swapped invoice incident.
✓Run #2The final response under 'Key Finding: Invoice Attachment Validation (ENG-187)' states 'This dormant ticket IS connected to the swapped invoices issue — I flagged it in #engineering on 4/14 after the second incident,' establishing the connection between the deprioritized ticket and the swapped invoice incidents.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly states 'Escalated ENG-187 (invoice attachment mismatch) to P1 with comment connecting it to the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident — this is the dormant ticket you remembered, and yes, the recent invoice swap incident is the same failure mode.' Both the dormant ticket and the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident are mentioned and connected.
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states 'ENG-187 is directly connected to the Palmetto Foundation invoice swap ($4,800 wrong invoice). It's the same failure mode as the earlier Terraform pricing leak.' This directly connects the dormant invoice attachment ticket to the recent invoice swap incident.
✓Run #5The final response has a dedicated 'Invoice Attachment Ticket (ENG-187)' section that states it is 'Connected to the Sunbelt/Palmetto swapped invoice incident' and describes it as a 'second pricing-exposure incident touching the same failure mode.'
✗Run #6The final response mentions ENG-187 as 'about invoice email mismatches' and notes 'there's been a second incident since it was deprioritized,' but never names Sunbelt or Palmetto. The agent searched for 'Sunbelt Palmetto invoice' and got no results, so it couldn't make this specific connection. The criterion requires referencing both the ticket and the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident specifically.
